content:
# Viscosity

**Summary**: A measure of a fluid's resistance to flow.

---

## How It Appears in the Thesis Collection

- [[2023-ahmed|Ahmed, 2023]] measured viscosity to determine the mobility control capabilities of the chemicals.

- [[2024-abdallah|Abdallah, 2024]] used viscosity measurement to characterize the rheological properties of emulsified TCFs and fracturing fluids.

## Scientific Context

- [[2023-ahmed|Ahmed, 2023]] Viscosity is critical for mobility control in chemical EOR.

- [[2024-abdallah|Abdallah, 2024]] used viscosity measurement to characterize the rheological properties of emulsified TCFs and fracturing fluids.

## Measurement and Characterization Methods

- [[2023-ahmed|Ahmed, 2023]] measured using a rheometer.

## Key Findings Across Sources

- The highest viscosity for GLDA was 738 cP at the working shear rate. ([[2023-ahmed|Ahmed, 2023, p. 23]])

- Viscosity was measured for 70% emulsified NH4Cl and NaNO2 in diesel with both 1% and 5% emulsifier concentrations across a temperature range of 25-90°C. ([[2024-abdallah|Abdallah, 2024, p. 71]])

## Contrasting Values and Interpretations

- [[2023-ahmed|Ahmed, 2023]] Values ranged from 325 cP to 1953 cP for pure VES.
